case.title: NATIONAL LABOR RELATIONS BOARD v. CLARK'S GAMBLE CORP.

opinion.h1: NATIONAL LABOR RELATIONS BOARD v. CLARK'S GAMBLE CORP., 396 U.S. 23 (1969)
opinion.h4: NATIONAL LABOR RELATIONS BOARD v. CLARK'S GAMBLE CORP., DBA CLARK'S DISCOUNT DEPARTMENT STORE, ET AL. ON PETITION FOR WRIT OF CERTIORARI TO THE UNITED STATES COURT OF APPEALS FOR THE SIXTH CIRCUIT No. 439. Decided November 10, 1969
opinion.p: Certiorari granted; 407 F.2d 199, vacated and remanded.
Solicitor General Griswold, Arnold Ordman, Dominick L. Manoli, Norton J. Come, and Leonard M. Wagman for petitioner.
Earle K. Shawe for respondent M. N. Landau Stores, Inc.

The petition for a writ of certiorari is granted, the judgment is vacated, and the case is remanded to the United States Court of Appeals for the Sixth Circuit for further consideration in light of NLRB v. Gissel Packing Co., 395 U.S. 575.
396 U.S. 23 CLINTON v. CALIFORNIA APPEAL FROM THE SUPREME COURT OF CALIFORNIA No. 632, Misc. Decided November 10, 1969
The appeal is dismissed for want of jurisdiction. Treating the papers whereon the appeal was taken as a petition for a writ of certiorari, certiorari is denied.
